title: Trigonometry, Vectors, and Periodic Systems · Decision Defense
summary: Plan bearings, slopes, cyclic demand, and resultant forces. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a navigation and periodic-demand model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use radians and the unit circle to define trigonometric functions and periodicity.
Apply right-triangle ratios with diagrams, units, and angle conditions.
Choose laws of sines or cosines and detect ambiguous cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate the unfamiliar quantity
steps.instruction: Perpendicular components are 3 units and 4 units. Calculate the resultant magnitude. Use the result to decide Sinusoidal Fit for a navigation and periodic-demand model.
steps.why: The Sinusoidal Fit result for a navigation and periodic-demand model is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use √(3² + 4²) and check that the result exceeds either component. Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Fit amplitude, period, phase, and midline to a periodic dataset.
